instruction: Make a Blueprint that has a Gate. Use an Open input to open the gate and print Gate is now open. Use a Close input to shut the gate and print Gate shut. On every Tick, send execution through the gate, and if it comes out the other side print Running.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateTest
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Open"]
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Gate is now open"]
NODE n4: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Gate shut"]
NODE n6: Event_Tick
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Running"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Open
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n7.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateTest
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Open"]
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Gate is now open"]
NODE n4: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Gate shut"]
NODE n6: Event_Tick
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Running"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Open
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n7.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateTest
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Open"]
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Gate is now open"]
NODE n4: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Close"]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Gate shut"]
NODE n6: Event_Tick
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Running"]

EXEC n1.Pressed -> n2.Open
EXEC n1.Pressed -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n2.Close
EXEC n4.Pressed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n7.Execute

instruction: Build a Blueprint with a Gate node. Wire BeginPlay to open the gate. Wire an overlap event to enter the gate. The gate exit should print Triggered. Wire a Toggle input to toggle the gate and print Toggled.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateOverlap
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Triggered"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Toggle"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Toggled"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Toggle
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateOverlap
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Triggered"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Toggle"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Toggled"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Toggle
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute
raw_output:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GateOverlap
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: Gate
NODE n3: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Triggered"]
NODE n5: Event_InputAction [ActionName="Toggle"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Toggled"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Open
EXEC n3.Then -> n2.Enter
EXEC n2.Exit -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n2.Toggle
EXEC n5.Pressed -> n6.Execute

instruction: Create a Blueprint with Health at 100. When the actor takes any damage, subtract it from Health, save the new value, and print a message showing how much damage was taken and what health remains.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageReport
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DamageReport
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
raw_output:

PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n5: PrintString

DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on BeginPlay does this math: start with 200, divide by 4, subtract 10, multiply by 3, and print the answer.

NODE n3: SubtractFloat [B=10.0]
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat [B=3.0]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.A [Float]

instruction: Build a Blueprint. When it spawns, compute: 50 plus 25 equals a sum. Multiply that sum by 2. Then subtract 30. Print the final number.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MathOps
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: AddFloat [A=50.0, B=25.0]
NODE n3: MultiplyFloat [B=2.0]
NODE n4: SubtractFloat [B=30.0]
NODE n5: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]

instruction: Make a pressure plate Blueprint. When something overlaps it, cast to see if it is a Character. If it is, set a Pressed boolean to true and print Activated. When the overlap ends, set Pressed to false and print Deactivated.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_FloorPlate
PARENT: Actor

VAR Pressed: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CastToCharacter
NODE n3: SetVar [Variable=Pressed, Value=true]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Activated"]
NODE n5: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Pressed, Value=false]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Deactivated"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.CastSucceeded -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n1.OtherActor -> n2.Object [Actor]
actual_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PressurePlate

NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Pressed, Value=false]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute

EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

instruction: Make a Blueprint that enables physics on BeginPlay. On every Tick, apply a downward force scaled by a GravityMultiplier variable defaulting to 3.0.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_HeavyGravity
PARENT: Actor

VAR GravityMultiplier: Float = 3.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetSimulatePhysics [bSimulate=true]
NODE n3: Event_Tick
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=GravityMultiplier]
NODE n5: MultiplyFloat [B=-980.0]
NODE n6: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n7: AddForce

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Z [Float]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Force [Vector]

instruction: Create a patrolling actor. It has two vector variables Start at (0,0,0) and End at (1000,0,0). On Tick, lerp between them using DeltaSeconds as alpha and set the actor location.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Patroller
PARENT: Actor

VAR Start: Vector = (0,0,0)
VAR End: Vector = (1000,0,0)

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Start]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=End]
NODE n4: VectorLerp
NODE n5: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n4.A [Vector]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.B [Vector]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n4.Alpha [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.NewLocation [Vector]

instruction: Build a damage area. When an actor enters, start a repeating timer called Hurt that fires every second. When they leave, stop the timer. The Hurt event subtracts 15 from Health and prints it.

instruction: Create a Blueprint that on overlap runs a Sequence with 3 outputs. First path prints Step 1, second path prints Step 2 after a 1 second delay, third path prints Step 3 after a 2 second delay.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TripleStep
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: Sequence
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Step 1"]
NODE n4: Delay [Duration=1.0]
NODE n5: PrintString [InString="Step 2"]
NODE n6: Delay [Duration=2.0]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Step 3"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.A -> n3.Execute
EXEC n2.B -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Completed -> n5.Execute
EXEC n2.C -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Completed -> n7.Execute

instruction: Make a moving platform. Use a float Timer starting at 0 and Speed at 1.0. Each tick, add DeltaSeconds times Speed to Timer. Use sine of Timer multiplied by 200 for the Z offset. Set actor location with X=0, Y=0, Z=offset.
expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Elevator
PARENT: Actor

VAR Timer: Float = 0.0
VAR Speed: Float = 1.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=Speed]
NODE n4: MultiplyFloat
NODE n5: AddFloat
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n7: Sin
NODE n8: MultiplyFloat [B=200.0]
NODE n9: MakeVector [X=0, Y=0]
NODE n10: SetActorLocation

EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n10.Execute

DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n4.A [Float]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.B [Float]
DATA n2.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.B [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.A [Float]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.Z [Float]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.NewLocation [Vector]
